title: AI Is Splitting Careers Into Two Tracks

claims.claim: PwC's 2026 Global AI Jobs Barometer analysed more than 1 billion job ads across six continents.
Professionalised roles are growing twice as fast as democratised roles, with 42% faster wage growth.
Fewer than 1% of workers need advanced AI skills; most need digital skills, data interpretation, managerial and human skills.

scenes.narration: AI isn't creating one job market. It's creating two.

visual.headline: AI is splitting careers in two

scenes.narration: In one track, AI makes your output easier to copy. In the other... it makes your judgment more valuable.

visual.verdict: One gets copied. One gets paid.
visual.subtitle: AI commoditises output — and rewards judgment.

scenes.narration: PwC scanned over a billion job ads. Roles built on expertise are growing twice as fast — with 42% faster wage growth.

visual.label: faster wage growth in expertise-driven roles
visual.subtitle: From 1 billion+ job ads across six continents.

scenes.narration: Here's the twist. Fewer than 1% of workers need advanced AI skills. Most need data fluency... and human judgment.

visual.subtitle: Fewer than 1% of workers need advanced AI skills.

scenes.narration: So don't just learn the tool. Move toward work where AI makes your judgment more valuable — not your output easier to copy.

visual.setup: Output gets copied. Judgment gets scarce.
visual.question: WHICH TRACK IS YOUR WORK ON?
visual.subtitle: Move where AI raises your value.

packaging.youtubeTitle: AI Is Splitting Careers Into Two Tracks
packaging.instagramCaption: AI isn't creating one job market — it's creating two. Expertise-driven roles are growing 2x faster, with 42% higher wage growth (PwC, 1B+ job ads). The edge isn't output. It's judgment. #ai #careers #futureofwork
